module_mood_contract: operational clarity — organisers feel they own the customer-side workspace, folder test, grouping, and readout path

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[6] (Phase 4. Rehearse organiser readout): mood_score=7. Line 160: 'Next, run the organiser synthesis exercise below on the rollout synthesis files in shared/' — 'below' is a dangling reference; no exercise appears below in this document. The organiser is mid-dry-run and hits a navigation gap, interrupting the operational clarity contract.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace 'the organiser synthesis exercise below' with an inline link to organisers-synthesize-rollout.md (or include the prompt directly), so Phase 4 completes without navigation exit.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 160: 'Next, run the organiser synthesis exercise below on the rollout synthesis files in shared/' — the readout dry-run forcing function is body-prose instruction pointing to an absent inline exercise rather than a fenced prompt the organiser can copy. A speed-reader working Phase 4 prompt-to-prompt hits the fenced 'create two fake syntheses' prompt then encounters no subsequent fenced prompt; the readout dry-run is unanchored in this file.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either include the organisers-synthesize-rollout.md prompt inline in Phase 4 (as a fenced block or a render-include link), or replace the body-prose pointer with a direct link that makes navigation deterministic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch named in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 4 ships a forcing function (open new Cowork session, create fake syntheses, run organiser readout) but the maintainer block names no failure mode or escape hatch for Phase 4. The watch-out in body ('The first readout may flatten the three cruxes into one bland theme') is placed correctly for the live room but no organiser-specific dry-run failure mode is documented. Phase 3 has a watch-out for Claude saving before showing the crux candidate — Phase 4 does not.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a Phase 4 failure mode to the maintainer block: dominant failure = organiser cannot locate the synthesis exercise (navigation gap); escape hatch = link or inline the prompt so Phase 4 is self-contained.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M4 teaching moment: 'customer-side operation is part of the system. The trainer can show the Bosser system; organisers prove and run the customer workspace.' This requires Phase 4 to complete cleanly — the organiser must run the readout dry-run. Phase 4's dangling 'exercise below' reference means the teaching moment does not land reliably without prior knowledge of the workbook structure. An organiser reading organisers-prepare-claude-basics.md in isolation (self-study, pre-workshop) cannot complete Phase 4 without navigating out of the file.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Inline the organiser synthesis exercise prompt or a direct render-include link in Phase 4 so the teaching moment fires without navigation dependency.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M4 Big Idea: 'The same workbook carries the organiser runbook, but organiser work stays separate from the participant path.' The file is entirely organiser-operational (5 phases), no participant-path content appears, and the section-level separation is maintained.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early / steal next module's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 is the final module in Claude Basics. No downstream module to protect.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with your neighbor,' 'share with the room,' 'compare with the person next to you' language detected. All body prose describes organiser operational steps. This is an organisers-only runbook — no cohort management language.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
Plug points are integration commitments; UX — operator can paste each prompt without inventing setup.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Fast-operator diagnostic: read Phase 4 prompt-to-prompt. After the fenced 'create two fake syntheses' prompt, the next instruction is body prose pointing to an absent exercise. The operator hits a 'wait, what?' moment — no fenced prompt to paste next. Phase 4 UX is broken for the fast-reading organiser. Separate from the mood finding — same root cause, different judge axis.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Fix is the same as rule_index 16 above: inline or link the synthesis prompt in Phase 4.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 is the final module. Phase 5 closes with a readiness checklist — a clean operational landing. No next module to hand off to. Strategy doc bridge: 'the workbook is one artifact for the customer, but the audience boundaries are explicit.' The checklist serves that boundary clarification.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood is not punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Mood is operational clarity throughout — no 'you did something wrong' register. The watch-out notes (Claude saving before showing crux candidate, first readout may flatten cruxes) are practical warnings, not corrective framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block carries a Core readiness test (line 188) but not three observable Monday-morning outcomes in the leap-test format. The readiness test names what passes, not what the organiser can do by next working day. The current quality state is 'draft' so this gap is documented, not blocking ship at this tier — but story judge flags it for the next quality level.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add three named leap-test outcomes to the maintainer block (e.g. 'shared/rollout-synthesis-rehearsal.md created and confirmed visible from a second machine', 'CLAUDE.md written at folder root with OneDrive consistency note', 'group driver identified and folder list prepared').

stealing_the_mood: Phase 4 (Rehearse organiser readout): mood_score=7 — dangling 'exercise below' reference forces navigation exit mid-dry-run; operational clarity contract interrupted. Fix: inline the synthesis prompt or provide a deterministic link.
